-# Contributing to AgentFlow
+# Contributing Guidelines
 
-Thank you for your interest in contributing to AgentFlow! This guide will help you get started.
+Thank you for considering contributing to our project! Here are some guidelines to help you get started.
 
-## Development Setup
+## Setting Up Your Development Environment
 
-### Prerequisites
+1. **Clone the repository**
+   ```bash
+   git clone https://github.com/your-repo/repo-name.git
+   cd repo-name
+   ```
 
-- Docker and Docker Compose
-- Node.js 20+ and pnpm 9+
-- Python 3.12+ and Poetry
-- Git
+2. **Install dependencies**
+   - For Python (backend):
+     ```bash
+     cd apps/api
+     poetry install
+     ```
+   - For Node.js (frontend):
+     ```bash
+     cd apps/web
+     pnpm install
+     ```
 
-### Getting Started
+## Coding Standards
 
-```bash
-# Clone the repo
-git clone https://github.com/codersanket/agentflow.git
-cd agentflow
-
-# Copy environment config
-cp .env.example .env
-
-# Start infrastructure (Postgres + Redis)
-docker compose -f infra/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
-
-# Install frontend dependencies
-pnpm install
-
-# Install backend dependencies
-cd apps/api && poetry install && cd ../..
-
-# Run database migrations
-cd apps/api && poetry run alembic upgrade head && cd ../..
-
-# Start the app (two terminals)
-pnpm dev:api     # Terminal 1: Backend on :8000
-pnpm dev:web     # Terminal 2: Frontend on :3000
-```
-
-## Code Style
-
-### Python (Backend)
-
-- **Formatter/Linter:** [Ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/)
-- **Line length:** 100 characters
-- **Type hints:** Required on all function signatures
-- **Imports:** `from __future__ import annotations` at the top of every file
-- **Async:** All I/O operations must use async/await
-
-```bash
-cd apps/api
-poetry run ruff check .      # Check lint errors
-poetry run ruff format .     # Auto-format
-```
-
-### TypeScript (Frontend)
-
-- **Linter:** ESLint (Next.js config)
-- **Components:** PascalCase, named exports (not default)
-- **Files:** kebab-case.tsx for components, kebab-case.ts for utilities
-- **State:** Zustand for cross-component, React state for local UI
-- **Forms:** react-hook-form + zod validation
-
-```bash
-cd apps/web
-pnpm lint
-```
+- Follow the PEP 8 style guide for Python code.
+- Use Prettier and ESLint for JavaScript/TypeScript code.
+- Write clear, concise commit messages.
+- Ensure all code changes are covered by tests.
 
 ## Pull Request Process
 
-### 1. Create a branch
-
-```bash
-git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
-# or
-git checkout -b fix/your-bug-fix
-```
-
-### 2. Make your changes
-
-- Write code following the style guidelines above
-- Add tests for new functionality
-- Update documentation if needed
-
-### 3. Test your changes
-
-```bash
-# Backend
-cd apps/api && poetry run pytest
-
-# Frontend
-cd apps/web && pnpm build
-```
-
-### 4. Commit
-
-Use clear, descriptive commit messages:
-
-```
-feat: add Notion integration
-fix: resolve token refresh race condition
-docs: update self-hosting guide for ARM64
-refactor: extract variable resolver into separate module
-```
-
-### 5. Open a Pull Request
-
-- Fill out the PR template
-- Link any related issues
-- Ensure CI passes
-- Request review from a maintainer
-
-## What to Contribute
-
-### Good First Issues
-
-Look for issues labeled `good-first-issue` — these are scoped tasks ideal for newcomers.
-
-### Integration Plugins
-
-Adding new integrations is one of the best ways to contribute. Each integration is self-contained in `apps/api/engine/tools/`:
-
-1. Create a new file (e.g., `notion.py`)
-2. Implement the `IntegrationTool` interface
-3. Register in the tool registry
-4. Add to the available integrations list
-
-### Bug Fixes
+1. **Fork the repository** and create your feature branch from `main`.
+   ```bash
+   git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
+   ```
 
-Found a bug? Open an issue first, then submit a PR with:
-- A description of the bug
-- Steps to reproduce
-- Your fix and any tests
+2. **Make your changes** and commit them.
 
-## Project Structure
+3. **Run tests** to ensure everything is working as expected.
+   - **Python tests:**
+     ```bash
+     cd apps/api
+     poetry run pytest
+     ```
+   - **JavaScript/TypeScript tests:**
+     ```bash
+     cd apps/web
+     pnpm test
+     ```
 
-```
-agentflow/
-├── apps/web/           # Next.js frontend
-│   ├── app/            # App Router pages
-│   ├── components/     # React components
-│   ├── stores/         # Zustand state stores
-│   └── lib/            # Utilities, API client
-├── apps/api/           # FastAPI backend
-│   ├── core/           # Config, DB, auth, dependencies
-│   ├── models/         # SQLAlchemy models
-│   ├── schemas/        # Pydantic request/response schemas
-│   ├── routers/        # API route handlers
-│   ├── services/       # Business logic
-│   ├── engine/         # Execution engine, providers, tools
-│   ├── workers/        # Celery background tasks
-│   └── migrations/     # Alembic database migrations
-├── infra/              # Docker files
-└── docs/               # Documentation
-```
+4. **Push to your fork** and [submit a pull request](https://github.com/your-repo/repo-name/compare).
 
-## Key Conventions
+5. After review, an admin will merge your changes.
 
-- **Multi-tenancy:** Every database query MUST be scoped by `org_id`
-- **No secrets in code:** Never commit `.env`, API keys, or credentials
-- **Async everywhere:** All backend I/O uses async/await
-- **Pydantic validation:** All API input is validated with Pydantic schemas
-- **UUID primary keys:** All database tables use UUID PKs
+## Communication
 
-## Questions?
+- Use issues to report bugs or request features.
+- Engage in discussions via pull requests for any questions or clarifications.
 
-Open a [GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/codersanket/agentflow/discussions) or file an issue. We're happy to help!
+We appreciate your contributions and look forward to collaborating with you!
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/docs/TESTING.md b/docs/TESTING.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b4bf3b5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/TESTING.md
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# Testing Documentation
+
+This document outlines the various testing strategies in place to ensure that all contributions meet the project's quality standards.
+
+## Testing New Documentation
+
+When new documentation is added, follow these steps to verify:
+
+1. **Spell and Grammar Check**
+    Ensure the document is free from spelling and grammatical errors. This can be validated using tools like Grammarly or language-specific linters.
+
+2. **Formatting**
+    Ensure that Markdown syntax is correct and that all sections are properly organized and visible without rendering issues.
+
+3. **Links and References**
+    Verify that all hyperlinks work as expected and point to the correct resources.
+
+4. **Consistency Checks**
+    Ensure that the documentation aligns with existing project guidelines and does not contradict any current practices or instructions.
+
+5. **Peer Review**
+    Ensure that at least one other contributor reviews the documentation to catch any issues the author might have missed.
+
+## PR Process
+
+Refer to the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) for details on setting up your environment and the PR process.

changed_files.content:
# Contributing Guidelines

Thank you for considering contributing to our project! Here are some guidelines to help you get started.

## Setting Up Your Development Environment

1. **Clone the repository**
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/your-repo/repo-name.git
   cd repo-name
   ```

2. **Install dependencies**
   - For Python (backend):
     ```bash
     cd apps/api
     poetry install
     ```
   - For Node.js (frontend):
     ```bash
     cd apps/web
     pnpm install
     ```

## Coding Standards

- Follow the PEP 8 style guide for Python code.
- Use Prettier and ESLint for JavaScript/TypeScript code.
- Write clear, concise commit messages.
- Ensure all code changes are covered by tests.

## Pull Request Process

1. **Fork the repository** and create your feature branch from `main`.
   ```bash
   git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
   ```

2. **Make your changes** and commit them.

3. **Run tests** to ensure everything is working as expected.
   - **Python tests:**
     ```bash
     cd apps/api
     poetry run pytest
     ```
   - **JavaScript/TypeScript tests:**
     ```bash
     cd apps/web
     pnpm test
     ```

4. **Push to your fork** and [submit a pull request](https://github.com/your-repo/repo-name/compare).

5. After review, an admin will merge your changes.

## Communication

- Use issues to report bugs or request features.
- Engage in discussions via pull requests for any questions or clarifications.

We appreciate your contributions and look forward to collaborating with you!

# Testing Documentation

This document outlines the various testing strategies in place to ensure that all contributions meet the project's quality standards.

## Testing New Documentation

When new documentation is added, follow these steps to verify:

1. **Spell and Grammar Check**
    Ensure the document is free from spelling and grammatical errors. This can be validated using tools like Grammarly or language-specific linters.

2. **Formatting**
    Ensure that Markdown syntax is correct and that all sections are properly organized and visible without rendering issues.

3. **Links and References**
    Verify that all hyperlinks work as expected and point to the correct resources.

4. **Consistency Checks**
    Ensure that the documentation aligns with existing project guidelines and does not contradict any current practices or instructions.

5. **Peer Review**
    Ensure that at least one other contributor reviews the documentation to catch any issues the author might have missed.

## PR Process

Refer to the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) for details on setting up your environment and the PR process.

pr_description.title: Add CONTRIBUTING.md with Contribution Guidelines
pr_description.body:
This PR includes the addition of a CONTRIBUTING.md file outlining guidelines for setting up the development environment, coding standards, and the pull request process. This document intends to assist new contributors in understanding the workflow and maintaining consistency across contributions.

Additionally, a TESTING.md file has been added under the docs directory, providing a detailed approach to verifying new documentation changes made to the project.
